identity_fields.instance_id: Unique ID for each deployment instance.
identity_fields.global_id: Stable UUID/ULID for objects that may sync across instances.
identity_fields.origin_instance_id: Instance that first created the object.
identity_fields.revision: Monotonic revision for mutable synced objects.
identity_fields.content_hash: Hash for immutable or append-only payloads.
identity_fields.visibility_scope: public/internal/restricted boundary used before any sync.

single_database.description: Public and intranet app instances point to one PostgreSQL database through a private network or VPN.
single_database.best_for: One trusted network or early deployment where operational simplicity matters.
risks: Public app must never expose database port
Intranet data boundary may be harder to reason about

database_replication.description: Use PostgreSQL physical or logical replication between databases.
database_replication.best_for: Read-only replicas or infrastructure teams comfortable operating PostgreSQL replication.
risks: Conflict handling is hard for two writable databases
Schema migration order must be strict
Sensitive intranet data may replicate unintentionally

app_level_outbox_inbox.description: Recommended for public plus intranet. Each app writes local DB; selected objects sync through signed export/import packages or internal API.
app_level_outbox_inbox.best_for: Clear security boundaries, one-way or controlled two-way sync, auditability.

intranet_to_public: non-sensitive data_source config changes if explicitly approved
taxonomy/domain pack updates if managed outside Git
aggregate source quality scores without user details if explicitly approved

sync_safety_rules: Objects with visibility_scope = restricted must never sync automatically.
Objects with visibility_scope = internal must not sync to public by default.
Secrets, tokens, cookies and credentials must be referenced by credential_ref and never included in sync packages.
Public-to-intranet sync is allowed for public raw/news/recommendation data.
Intranet-to-public sync requires explicit admin approval and must be aggregate or non-sensitive config only.
